rationale: Two critical verification-readiness gaps block PASS: REQ-006 (sortOrder passthrough) is mapped only to a determinism PROP that cannot detect a hardcoded sortOrder, and REQ-011 (case-insensitive substring search across body+frontmatter) is mapped to PROPs whose stated invariants do not exercise substring, case-insensitivity, or scope. Eight major findings further undermine coverage: REQ-007/REQ-010/REQ-014's matrix entries are one-sided, REQ-003's fail-fast-across-mixed-validity invariant is unverified, PROP-011 is mis-tiered (claims compile-time verification of a runtime equality), and on the fidelity side REQ-012 invents an undocumented NoteId-lexicographic tiebreak, REQ-011 silently narrows the documented `body+frontmatter` scope to tag names, and tag-array deduplication after normalization is left unspecified. Two minor findings note inconsistencies between Bun-pinned perf bounds and a vitest-based harness, an unverified claim that vitest is already present in package.json, and an undocumented `searchTextRaw.trim()` rule that contradicts ui-fields.md's `直流し` directive. Lean mode tolerates ≤2 majors but this iteration has 8 majors and 2 criticals — exceeds the FAIL threshold on both axes.
